macro.takeaway: The latest U.S. data shows consumers still spending, which can keep growth resilient but may also keep inflation pressure from cooling quickly.

gold.takeaway: Gold ended the week slightly lower after a mid‑week dip, but it is still being supported by inflation worries and geopolitical uncertainty.

china.takeaway: China A‑shares were roughly flat on the week; the latest official data shows retail sales up 1.9% (Jan–Apr), suggesting a modest (not booming) consumption recovery.

source.name: China National Bureau of Statistics press release

thailand.takeaway: Thailand’s market was basically unchanged on the day, and the BoT is still holding rates at 1.00%, which keeps borrowing costs relatively supportive but leaves limited room if growth slows.

us.takeaway: U.S. stocks finished higher for the week while the Fed is still on hold—markets are effectively betting inflation won’t re‑accelerate enough to force new hikes.
